name: Dr Temuulen "Teki" Sankey
school: School of Informatics, Computing, Cyber Systems
focus: Remote sensing • Ecosystem change • UAV and LiDAR mapping
bio: Dr. Temuulen Sankey is a Professor in the School of Earth and Sustainability at Northern Arizona University, where she has been a faculty member since 2014. Her research focuses on applying remote sensing technologies to better understand and monitor environmental change, particularly in arid and semi-arid ecosystems. Dr. Sankey specializes in multi-scale vegetation mapping using satellite and aerial imagery, as well as change detection methods for long-term ecosystem monitoring. Her work integrates optical and thermal infrared remote sensing, LiDAR, and Geographic Information Systems (GIS), along with spatial statistics and predictive modeling, including machine learning applications in environmental science. She is also a leader in the use of UAV (drone) technology, developing monitoring protocols and high-resolution mapping techniques to assess vegetation structure, composition, and real-time environmental conditions. Her research addresses key issues in dryland ecosystems, including vegetation dynamics, climate change impacts, restoration of disturbed landscapes, and fire effects in grassland and shrubland systems. Dr. Sankey earned her Ph.D. in Geography from Arizona State University, her M.S. in Environmental Remote Sensing from the University of Arizona, and her B.S. in Geography from the National University of Mongolia.
